steps.title: Identify the triangle structure and known information
steps.content:
The triangle has a right angle at $S$. We know:
  • Hypotenuse $TR = 84$
  • One leg $RS = \sqrt{167}$
To find $\sin R$, we need the side opposite angle $R$ (which is $TS$) and the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Find the missing leg using the Pythagorean theorem
Apply the sine ratio and compute the answer
steps.content:
For angle $R$, the opposite side is $TS$ and the hypotenuse is $TR$:
$$\sin R = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{TS}{TR} = \frac{83}{84}$$This equals approximately \$0.988$. That matches choice 83/84 (or \$0.988$ in decimal form).
